title: Device Control
matter_help: Pair Matter / Thread devices via QR or manual setup code. Requires the matter-server companion container — start it with "docker compose --profile matter up -d". WiFi Matter devices work directly; native Thread devices additionally need the OpenThread Border Router (--profile thread).
matter_no_devices: No Matter devices yet — pair one above.
matter_confirm_remove: Remove this Matter device? It will be decommissioned and forgotten by the hub.
matter_fill_required: Setup code and device name are required.
providers_help: Each provider is a smart-device protocol library (Tuya, Gree, Philips Hue, ESPHome, …). Built-in providers ship with the container; opt-in extras are installed on demand without restarting. State is persisted in the registry DB so power loss never wipes your configuration.
prov_confirm_disable: Disable this provider? Existing devices using it will go offline until you re-enable.
no_devices: No devices yet — add them via the Tuya Cloud Wizard tab.
edit_help: English forms are auto-generated by LLM from the displayed name and room. They drive voice command patterns and are not editable manually — type meaningful names in your own language instead.
wizard_help_intro: Pair your Smart Life account with SelenaCore using your app authorization code — no developer account needed.
wizard_step_1: Open the Smart Life (or Tuya Smart) app on your phone
wizard_step_2: Tap Me in the bottom bar → ⚙️ icon top-right → Authorization code
wizard_step_3: Copy the 6-8 character code (valid for ~10 minutes)
wizard_step_4: Paste the code below and click "Start"
wizard_step_5: SelenaCore will show a QR. Tap "+" in Smart Life → Scan → point at the QR
wizard_step_6: Tap "Authorize" in the app. SelenaCore will automatically import all your devices.
wizard_confirm_disconnect: Disconnect Tuya Smart Life? All linked devices will stop working until you reconnect.
wizard_scan_instructions: Scan this QR with the Smart Life app (+ → Scan):
wizard_waiting: Waiting for you to scan and confirm in the app…
wizard_no_devices: No devices found in your Smart Life account. Add a device in the app first.
gree_help: Discover Gree-protocol air conditioners on your local network (Pular, Gree, Cooper&Hunter, EWT and other Gree OEMs). The unit must already be on your Wi-Fi — pair it with the Gree+ / Ewpe Smart app first, then click "Scan". No cloud account is used.
gree_no_devices: No Gree-compatible units found on the LAN. Make sure the AC is powered on, on the same Wi-Fi as this hub, and not in pairing mode.
hue_help: Add Philips Hue lights via the standard Hue REST API. Works with real Hue Bridges and Hue-compatible emulators (SDS, diyHue, etc.). Enter the API host URL and a token (username). Light IDs are visible via GET /api/<token>/lights.
hue_no_found: No lights found on this bridge.
hue_no_devices: No Hue devices yet — discover and import above.
hue_fill_required: API host and token are required.
esp_help: Connect ESPHome devices over the LAN via the native API. Enter the device IP (port 6053 by default). The driver will auto-discover all entities on the device when it connects.
esp_no_devices: No ESPHome devices yet — add one above.
esp_fill_required: Device IP and name are required.
z2m_help: Discover and import devices from a running Zigbee2MQTT instance. Enter the MQTT broker address and click "Discover" to scan zigbee2mqtt/bridge/devices.
z2m_no_found: No Zigbee2MQTT devices found. Check that Z2M is running and MQTT broker is reachable.
z2m_no_devices: No Zigbee2MQTT devices yet — discover and import above.
